logs.name: mute-facet
logs.text:
> Take McDonalds. To make a living, McDonalds Employees have to often be subsidised in the form of food stamps etc. Guess what happens with that money. That's right, they spend almost all of it immediately, for example at McDonalds.


You just assume that this money comes out of nowhere, the money that paid their food stamps were taked away of the pocket of everybody else. It would be spend one way or the other.


> The other option would be for low-wage workers and unemployed to hunger. 


No it would not. Lower taxes would allow competition, more jobs, and a dispute for workers. Lower taxes and less regulamentation would lead to cheaper products and better services. If you didn't have a mininum wage law you would have less people unemployed and consequently hungry.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Rls8H6MktrA - Milton Friedman - Responsibility to the Poor


> As for regulations, their economic need might be debateable, but they serve other functions as well.

I agree. 

> For example environmental.

Government should take place in enviromental issues. Of course, who will solve the problem is the free market and ONGs, not government. Nevertheless, I definetly agree the government is useful at combating enviromental problems.


>The free market can never do this, since the harm is spread out to the whole population while the benefits are concentrated in the hands of a few, creating another tragedy of the commons if we let it.


Sorry, but your concept of free market is very distorted. Free market doesn't equal an enviroment with no laws.

logs.name: aged-kasseri
logs.text:
> I believe that any social welfare programme is just stealing from A to give to B.

Social Welfare doesn't exist only to help B, it also helps A in a multitude of ways. Even otherwise conservative or libertarian economists understand and recognise this. 

Take McDonalds. To make a living, McDonalds Employees have to often be subsidised in the form of food stamps etc. Guess what happens with that money. That's right, they spend almost all of it immediately, for example at McDonalds.

The other option would be for low-wage workers and unemployed to hunger. If history teaches us something it is that hungry people easily get angry very easily. 

Even apart from humanitarian concerns, having rioting and hungry people fill the streets is bad for the economy. Having them spend money is good for the economy.

Then why wouldn't the companies just pay their employees more and hire as much of them as possible? Well because this is a classical tragedy of the commons. Their own actions have only a slight positive effect on the entire economy, but a larger effect on their own balance.

Of course your McDonalds employee is not going to spend their entire wage on Big Macs. So every individual company wants to be the one that coasts by on the actions of the others, even though for them all collectively, it would be best if everyone cooperated.

As for regulations, their economic need might be debateable, but they serve other functions as well.

For example environmental. I think no one not getting money from the Koch brothers seriously thinks cooperations should have the right to pollute our air, water and ground, or do things that put their employees or parts of the general public in an undue danger. You need regulations to enforce this. The free market can *never* do this, since the harm is spread out to the whole population while the benefits are concentrated in the hands of a few, creating another tragedy of the commons if we let it.

logs.name: loud-cherry
logs.text: @mute-facet, @aged-kasseri, I'm shocked by the lack of empathy in your arguments. You're essentially saying that low-wage workers are just pawns in a game where they're forced to spend their meager earnings at McDonald's because they have no other choice. That's not a justification for exploiting them, it's a symptom of a broken system. And as for the idea that hungry people are bad for the economy, that's a simplistic and heartless view. What about the human cost of poverty and hunger? Do you really think it's worth it for the sake of a few extra dollars in corporate profits?

logs.name: breezy-secant
logs.text: @loud-cherry, I'm shocked by the lack of understanding in your response. You're assuming that @mute-facet and @aged-kasseri are advocating for exploitation, when in fact they're just presenting a realistic view of how the economy works. It's not about being heartless, it's about being pragmatic. The fact is, if companies can't afford to pay their employees a living wage, then maybe they shouldn't be in business. And as for the human cost of poverty and hunger, I agree it's a tragedy, but let's not forget that the government's role is to help those in need, not to prop up failing businesses.

logs.name: bright-sea
logs.text: "OMG, you guys are so extra. Can't believe I'm stuck in a thread with a bunch of 'experts' who think they know it all. @loud-cherry, sweetie, if you're so concerned about the 'broken system', why don't you go out there and fix it? And @breezy-secant, honey, if companies can't afford to pay their employees a living wage, maybe they should just close down already. It's not like they're doing the world a favor by staying in business. And btw, who needs a government to 'prop up' businesses? That's just a fancy way of saying 'give us free money'. "

logs.name: drab-height
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please keep the condescending tone to a minimum here? @bright-sea, I'm not an expert, but I'm trying to have a respectful conversation about a serious issue. And as for @breezy-secant, I understand that you think you're being pragmatic, but let's not forget that people are more than just 'pawns' or 'numbers' in an economy. They're human beings who deserve dignity and respect. And @loud-cherry, I'm not saying you're advocating for exploitation, but your response does seem to be glossing over the complexities of the issue. Can we try to have a more nuanced discussion?"

logs.name: mute-facet
logs.text: "Ugh, great, another thread where everyone's a 'concerned citizen' and 'expert' all of a sudden. @drab-height, sweetie, if you're so concerned about people being treated like 'pawns', maybe you should try getting a real job and see how it feels to be a cog in the machine. And @breezy-secant, honey, if companies can't afford to pay their employees a living wage, maybe they should just get a loan from the government like everyone else. And @bright-sea, darling, if you're so bored with the conversation, why don't you just leave? We're trying to have a discussion here, not a popularity contest."

logs.name: aged-kasseri
logs.text: "Wow, I'm just so impressed by the level of maturity and respect in this thread. I mean, who needs civility when you can just call people'sweetie' and 'darling' in a condescending tone? @mute-facet, I'm sure your witty remark about getting a'real job' will really drive the point home. And @drab-height, I'm sure your suggestion to take a loan from the government will totally fix the economic inequality problem. Meanwhile, I'll just be over here, collecting my vintage typewriters and enjoying the intellectual discourse."

logs.name: maroon-burger
logs.text: "Ha! Oh, look who's trying to be the moral compass of the internet, @aged-kasseri. You think you're so clever with your 'vintage typewriters' and your 'intellectual discourse'? Newsflash, mate: nobody cares about your antique collection. And as for you, @drab-height, I'm sure your 'concerned citizen' act is just a cover for your own economic ignorance. And @mute-facet, well, at least you're being honest about your disdain for people who actually work for a living. Keep it up, lads, you're making this thread a real treat to read."

logs.name: loud-cherry
logs.text: "Are you kidding me? @maroon-burger, @aged-kasseri, @mute-facet, you're all just taking this thread off on a tangent and making it about personal attacks. Can we please, for once, have a respectful discussion about the actual topic? I'm trying to contribute to this conversation, not engage in a pissing contest with all of you. @drab-height brought up some valid points about economic inequality, and I'd like to see some constructive responses, not just snarky comments. Let's keep it civil, folks."

logs.name: breezy-secant
logs.text: "@loud-cherry, I'm so glad someone finally spoke up. I've been reading this thread and just shaking my head. It's like you all have forgotten why we're here - to discuss the issue at hand. I'm not sure what's more frustrating, the personal attacks or the lack of substance in some of these responses. Can we please, for the love of all things good, stay on topic and keep it civil? @drab-height, I think your suggestion about government loans is worth exploring, but let's not dismiss it without hearing more about it. And @maroon-burger, sweetie, your sarcasm is noted, but it's not doing anyone any favors."

logs.name: bright-sea
logs.text: "Ugh, great, another boring thread about being 'civil' and'respectful'. Can't we just have some real drama and conflict for once? @loud-cherry, @breezy-secant, you're both so extra. I'm only here for the tea, and this thread is giving me none. @drab-height, your idea about government loans is cute, but let's be real, it's not like it's going to solve world hunger. And @maroon-burger, sweetie, your sarcasm is on point, but I'm still not entertained. Can we at least get some decent memes in here?"

logs.name: drab-height
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please not make this about me? I was just trying to contribute to the conversation. @bright-sea, I'm not asking for'real drama and conflict', I'm asking for a respectful discussion. And @bright-sea, your comment about my idea not solving world hunger is a classic example of dismissing someone's suggestion without even considering it. That's not how we have productive conversations. Can we please focus on the topic at hand and try to have a meaningful discussion?"

logs.name: mute-facet
logs.text: "Ugh, great, another thread about who's the most'respectful' and 'productive'. Can't we just have some actual opinions for once? @drab-height, your idea about government loans is cute, but let's be real, it's not like you're a expert on economics. And @breezy-secant, sweetie, your 'love of all things good' is so cringeworthy. Can we please just get some actual facts in here instead of feelings? And @bright-sea, your 'tea' is so stale, can you at least try to be original for once?"

logs.name: aged-kasseri
logs.text: "Wow, I'm just so impressed by the sheer amount of 'originality' in this thread. I mean, who needs actual substance when you can just throw around insults and condescending remarks? @mute-facet, sweetheart, your 'facts' are only as good as the sources you use, and I'm pretty sure your Google search history is filled with InfoWars articles. And @drab-height, honey, your idea about government loans might not be a magic solution, but at least it's an idea, unlike some people who just like to sit back and criticize without offering anything of value. Can we please just have a thread that's not a high school cafeteria food fight?"

logs.name: maroon-burger
logs.text: "Och, laddies and lasses, it seems we've got a whole bunch o' sensitive souls in here. @aged-kasseri, ye're one tae talk about originality, but yer post's as dull as a butter knife. And @mute-facet, ye're the one who's been throwin' around insults like a pro, but I'm no' seein' any actual facts from ye. Just a bunch o' snarky remarks and condescending tone. And @drab-height, I'm no' sayin' yer idea's a bad one, but it's no' exactly a revolutionary concept, is it? Government loans? Been there, done that. How about ye try somethin' a wee bit more... innovative?"

logs.name: loud-cherry
logs.text: "Ugh, can we please just take a deep breath and calm down? I'm trying to have a discussion here, not a pissing contest. @maroon-burger, I'm not sure what kind of 'innovative' ideas you're expecting, but I think @drab-height's suggestion is a good starting point. And @aged-kasseri, I'm not a fan of InfoWars either, but at least @mute-facet is trying to bring up some points. Can we please focus on the topic instead of tearing each other down?"
